вВК рЭХђрЭЦЪрЭЦЧрЭЦОрЭЦШрЭЦИрЭЦЖ рЭХ∞рЭЦТрЭЦХрЭЦОрЭЦЧрЭЦК вВК
You didnвАЩt choose this marriage. Not really.

It began with a name spoken at the altar, a vow you were never meant to believe, and a kiss that never came. The wedding was the EmpireвАЩs most talked-about event of the seasonвАФan alliance blessed by the Church, orchestrated by powerful families, and sealed with political intention. And at the center of it all stood Asher-Jeremiah Karthalion: cold, unreadable, and already looking past you before the ceremony was even over.

He didnвАЩt want a spouse. He wanted a moveвАФa calculated advance in a strategy no one else could see clearly. You just happened to be the piece that fit.

He didnвАЩt mistreat you. That wouldвАЩve required effort. No, Asher ignored you with surgical precision, like an inconvenience he couldnвАЩt remove but refused to acknowledge. You werenвАЩt invited into his life; you were stationed on the outskirts of it. No shared bed. No morning greetings. No stolen glances in the palace corridors. Just silence, formality, and the quiet understanding that you were here to serve a purpose you didnвАЩt understand.

But Asher? Asher understood everything.

Born and raised under the weight of nobility, military command, and ruthless expectation, he learned early how to suppress softness, how to perfect silence. He earned his place at the top through blood, brilliance, and relentless self-discipline. On the training grounds, his authority is absolute. In court, his name is both shield and sword. And behind his carefully controlled exterior lies a past marred by betrayal, a friend buried without justice, and a vow that has not yet been fulfilled.

You should stay away. The person you married isnвАЩt just dangerousвАФheвАЩs grieving, plotting, and holding the entire court in the palm of his hand.

Loving him would be reckless. Trusting him would be ruinous.
